summary: Retest of frontend after critical fixes to AppState (memoized update/updateSection/reset via useCallback + useMemo) and ReviewQueue (removed redundant update() inside load()). All previously-broken flows now PASS: (1) /review/uae-distributor-outreach renders 2 draft cards stably with 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests, and 0 'Maximum update depth' / 'ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES' messages. After initial load there are no further GETs to /api/campaigns/<id> in a 3s window, confirming the loop is gone. (2) Clicking draft-approve-draft-1 succeeded; PATCH /api/campaigns/.../approve returned 200; Approved counter incremented 0->1; status bar showed 'Draft draft-1 approved.'. (3) Editing draft-2 subject and clicking draft-save-draft-2 succeeded; PATCH .../edit returned 200; an 'edited' badge appeared on the card. (4) /leads -> click leads-generate-drafts navigated to /review/uae-distributor-outreach and loaded 2 draft cards immediately with no console errors. (5) /campaigns renders the table with both seeded campaigns (uae-distributor-outreach + test-uae-outreach) including drafts/approved counters. (6) localStorage state persistence verified: editing company name to 'PersistTest Co' on /company, navigating to /campaign and back preserved the value. Backend was not retested per instructions (was 100% green in iteration 1).

minor.issue: Carry-over from iteration 1: response body returns drafts with empty draft_id (''). Frontend works around it by re-fetching via GET. Upstream outreach_mvp serialization issue, not blocking.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Carry-over: fix POST /api/campaigns/draft to populate draft_id in the response so non-browser API consumers don't need a second GET.

(Nit) Status bar text persists previous message after a subsequent save action because it is parent-level state and the save handler updates it but a later onReload may overwrite via re-render race; observed status remained 'Draft draft-1 approved.' after editing draft-2. The 'edited' badge correctly appeared so the save itself worked. Cosmetic only.
critical_code_review_comments: AppState.jsx fix is correct: update/updateSection/reset wrapped in useCallback([]) and value wrapped in useMemo([state, ...]) makes context value stable across renders.

ReviewQueue.jsx fix is correct: load() no longer calls update() inside the fetch path; the dedicated useEffect on paramId handles currentCampaignId sync. load is memoized only on [campaignId] which is now stable.

Minor: in DraftCard, after successful saveEdits/approve we call onReload() which re-fetches the entire campaign. This is fine for current scale (<=10 drafts) but could be replaced with returning the updated draft to update local state more efficiently.

Minor: ReviewQueue passes a new inline onChange function to every DraftCard on each render; combined with DraftCard's useEffect on draft.subject/draft.body, this can clobber user edits if the parent re-renders with stale draft data. Today it only fires on legitimate reloads so it is OK, but worth noting.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None this iteration. Previously-seeded 'test-uae-outreach' campaign still present at /app/campaign_runs/test-uae-outreach.json. localStorage on the browser used in tests now contains company.name='PersistTest Co' from the persistence test - main agent or next tester may want to reset by clearing localStorage key 'lea-app-state-v1' or by using app's reset action.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Critical infinite-loop bug confirmed fixed. All draft review/approve/edit flows working end-to-end against the public preview URL. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_outreach_api.py (12/12 passing in iteration 1). If retesting, also clear browser localStorage key 'lea-app-state-v1' to reset company.name back to 'Acme Rubber Works'.
rca_of_the_issue: Iteration 1 RCA validated. Fixes applied in iteration 2 (memoization in AppState + removal of update() from ReviewQueue.load()) resolve the runaway useCallback->useEffect->setState cycle. Verified by (a) zero console errors of type 'Maximum update depth exceeded', (b) zero ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES, (c) network monitor counted zero repeat GETs to /api/campaigns/<id> after the initial load in a 3-second observation window, (d) Approve and Save edits PATCH calls now succeed in-browser with no 'Network Error'.
